specific-instructions.name: Project Instructions
specific-instructions.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/instructions" and put the instructions.

header.name: description
header.formHint: A short description of the project that will be used on the instructions template.

header.name: applyTo
header.formHint: A short description of what the instructions apply to.

body.name: mainInstructions
body.formHint: The main instructions for the project. This section can be used on the instructions template to provide detailed guidance and context for the project.

body.name: repositoryName
body.formHint: The name of the repository where the instructions will be used. This variable can be used on the instructions template to customize the instructions based on the repository name.

specific-instructions.name: Context Instructions

custom.name: Context
custom.hint: Prefix of the file name that will contain the context instructions. Use this value instead of the default context instructions file.

specific-instructions.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/instructions" and put the instructions. You need a main file with the name "copilot-instructions.md".

agent-instructions.name: Agent

custom.name: Context
custom.hint: Prefix of the file name to replace the agent name. Use this value instead of the default agent name.

agent-instructions.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/agents" and put the agent file.

header.name: description
header.formHint: A short description of the agent that will be used on the agent template.

header.name: argument-hint:
header.formHint: The inputs this agent expects, e.g., "a task to implement" or "a question to answer".

header.name: tools
header.formHint: Specify the tools this agent can use. If not set, all enabled tools are allowed.

header.name: mcp-servers
header.formHint: The MCPs online or local that only this agent will have access to. Must be in yml format.

body.name: role
body.formHint: Describe the role of the agent in second person. This is important to set the right context for the agent and guide its behavior. For example, "You are a senior developer with expertise in React and Node.js" or "You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about the project".

body.name: Repository Name
body.description: The name of the repository where the agent will be used. This variable can be used on the agent template to customize the agent based on the repository name.

skills.name: Skill
skills.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/skills/[skill_name]/SKILL.md" and put the skill file.

prompts.title: Prompt

custom.name: Context
custom.hint: Prefix of the file name to replace the prompt name. Use this value instead of the default prompt name.

prompts.installation: Create a subfolder on repository called ".github/prompts/[prompt_name]/PROMPT.md" and put the prompt file.

specific-instructions.name: Agent
specific-instructions.installation: Check your tools documentation for installation instructions. Mostly is just put on the project root.

skills.name: Skill
skills.installation: Check your tools documentation for installation instructions. Mostly is just put on the project root.

specific-instructions.title: Repository Instructions
specific-instructions.installation: On Firebase Studio or Gemini CLI, put the instructions file in the root.

agent-instructions.title: Assistant
agent-instructions.installation: On your project using ChatGPT, go to Assistants page, click on Create and paste your file content on "System instructions" box.

specific-instructions.title: Codex Instructions
specific-instructions.installation: On Codex, open Settings, on General, paste the file content on the "Personal Instructions" field.

agent-instructions.name: Agent main Context
agent-instructions.installation: On your Agent Zero instance, create a folder with your agent name and paste this file inside.

body.name: description
body.formHint: Describe the role of the agent in second person. This is important to set the right context for the agent and guide its behavior. For example, "You are a senior developer with expertise in React and Node.js" or "You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about the project".

body.name: actions
body.formHint: Describe the actions the agent can perform in second person. This is important to set your agent more specific. For example, "You can create and manage tasks in the project management tool" or "You can provide detailed explanations about the codebase".

agent-instructions.name: Agent Main Role
agent-instructions.installation: On your Agent Zero instance, create a subfolder "prompts" on your agent folder with your agent name and paste this file inside.

body.name: role
body.formHint: Describe the role of the agent in second person. This is important to set the right context for the agent and guide its behavior. For example, "You are a senior developer with expertise in React and Node.js" or "You are a helpful assistant that answers questions about the project".

body.name: actions
body.formHint: Describe the actions the agent can perform in second person. This is important to set your agent more specific. For example, "You can create and manage tasks in the project management tool" or "You can provide detailed explanations about the codebase".
